# Copilot Instructions — reidcooper/resume

This repository contains Reid Cooper's personal resume, written in LaTeX.

## Files

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `reid_cooper_resume.tex` | **Source of truth.** All content edits go here. |
| `reid_cooper_resume.pdf` | Auto-generated by CI — never edit manually. |
| `reid_cooper_resume.md` | Auto-generated by CI — never edit manually. |
| `reid_cooper_resume.txt` | Auto-generated by CI — never edit manually. |
| `resume_preview_p1.png` / `resume_preview_p2.png` | Auto-generated by CI — never edit manually. |
| `scripts/tex_to_formats.py` | Generates the `.md` and `.txt` outputs from the `.tex` source. |
| `.github/workflows/docker-image.yml` | CI: builds PDF, preview images, and text formats on every push to master. |

## Editing the resume

- Edit **`reid_cooper_resume.tex` only**.
- After pushing to `master`, CI automatically rebuilds the PDF, both preview images, and the `.md`/`.txt` exports via the `docker-image.yml` workflow.
- Do not manually edit `.pdf`, `.png`, `.md`, or `.txt` files.

## LaTeX structure

```latex
% Main company heading (first role or only role at a company)
\resumeSubheading{Company Name}{City, ST}{Job Title}{Mon YYYY - Mon YYYY}

% Additional roles at the same company (no repeated company name)
\resumeSubSubheading{Job Title}{Mon YYYY - Mon YYYY}

% Bullet point under a role
\resumeItem{Bullet Title}{Description of achievement or impact.}

% Bullet in Apps / Projects sections
\resumeSubItem{Project Name}{Description.}
```

Sections in order: Summary → Education → Experience → Apps → Projects → Technical Skills.

## Content conventions

- **Tone:** Staff Engineer voice — lead with org-level impact, decisions made, and measurable outcomes.
- **Framing:** What did you own, decide, and deliver? Avoid describing what a technology *is*; describe what *you* built, led, or achieved with it.
- **Tense:** Past tense for all roles except the current one (Staff Engineer, Apr 2025 – Present).
- **Titles:** `\resumeItem` titles are bold — keep them short (1–3 words), descriptive, and scannable.
- **Metrics:** Include numbers wherever possible (e.g., "8 million videos", "2ms response time", "27k+ RPM").

## Known TODOs in the resume

- The **Staff Engineer → File Migration Tiger Team** bullet has a `TODO` placeholder for cost savings. Leave it until Reid provides the number.
- The **Senior Software Engineer II → Revenue Initiatives** bullet has a `TODO` to locate revenue impact figures.

## Personal website sync

The personal website lives at `https://me.reidcooper.dev` (repo: `reidcooper/reidcooper.github.io`).

**After any resume session that changes content, always check whether the website needs updating.**

The website mirrors a subset of the resume:
- `Languages & Skills` → Skills section in `index.html` (Languages, Technical, Core Skills, AI Tools lines)
- `Apps / Projects` → Apps & Projects section in `index.html`
- `Experience` → Experience section in `index.html` (company + role only, no bullets)

**If the local repo is not present** at `/Users/reid/code/reidcooper.github.io`, prompt the user:
```sh
git clone git@github.com:reidcooper/reidcooper.github.io.git /Users/reid/code/reidcooper.github.io
```

When syncing, keep the site's minimal terminal aesthetic — no structural changes, just content updates.

## Building locally

```sh
bash build.sh  # requires Docker
```
